summary: Iteration 62 — Stripe SaaS subscription billing flow E2E (backend + frontend). 16/16 backend pytest tests PASSED, all auth paths and webhook simulations work. Frontend dashboard banner, subscribe-now toast (showing 'Invalid API Key provided: sk_test_****gent'), and active-row Manage billing · Cancel all verified. State restored to trial.
critical_code_review_comments: subscriptions.py imports 'stripe' inside each handler — fine for now but consider module-level import for consistency.

POST /api/stripe/webhook in DEV (no STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET) accepts unsigned payloads via stripe.Event.construct_from — code path correctly logs a WARNING. When STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_test was set in .env and backend restarted, unsigned POST returned 400 'Invalid payload' (because stripe.Webhook.construct_event raises ValueError before SignatureVerificationError when sig header is empty). Behavior is still correct — request is rejected — but the detail message is 'Invalid payload' instead of 'Invalid signature'. Acceptable, both are 400.
AdminDashboard.handleCancelSubscription uses window.confirm — works but a custom AlertDialog would be more consistent with the rest of the UI (minor UX).

platform_owner attempting /api/stripe/create-checkout is rejected by require_admin with 403 BEFORE the explicit 'Platform owner cannot subscribe' 400 check — the 400 branch in the handler is therefore unreachable. Either drop the 400 check (current behavior is fine) or change the dependency to get_current_user and let the 400 branch run. Functionally correct either way.
seed_data_creation: Test cleanup unsets stripe_customer_id, stripe_subscription_id, subscription_cancel_at_period_end, subscription_cancels_at, last_payment_at, cancelled_at, activated_at on rest_demo_1 and resets subscription_status to 'trial'. Verified clean post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stripe key in /app/backend/.env is sk_test_emergent placeholder — outbound Stripe calls return 'Invalid API Key' which is EXPECTED. Webhook simulations directly POST to /api/stripe/webhook so they don't hit Stripe. trial_days_left for rest_demo_1 is 0 because trial_ends_at=2026-04-05 has already passed (server clock is Apr 29 2026). The 'amber' tone banner shows for trial. After running webhook simulations, restore via PUT /api/subscriptions/{id} {status:trial} as platform_owner AND $unset stripe_* fields directly in mongo (the PUT endpoint doesn't clear them).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All endpoints work as designed. Auth paths: unauth=401/403, staff=403, platform_owner=403 (require_admin), admin=200 or 500 'Invalid API Key' (placeholder key — expected). Webhook: checkout.session.completed flips status to 'active' and persists stripe_customer_id/subscription_id; invoice.payment_succeeded inserts subscription_payment_succeeded notification and updates last_payment_at; customer.subscription.deleted flips status to 'cancelled'. Frontend toast renders 'Invalid API Key provided: sk_test_****gent' on subscribe attempt (no navigation). Active state row with manage-billing-link + cancel-subscription-link renders correctly post-activation.
